```{r}
question_binary_p_text <- function(data, group_col, outcome_col) {
  tab <- table(group = data[[group_col]], outcome = data[[outcome_col]] > 0)
  if (nrow(tab) < 2 || ncol(tab) < 2) {
    return("unavailable")
  }
  fmt_p(suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab, correct = FALSE)$p.value))
}

question_pred_ad_p_text <- function(code_value) {
  x <- question_events %>%
    filter(as.character(current_predicted_triage) %in% c("A", "D")) %>%
    mutate(current_predicted_triage = droplevels(current_predicted_triage))
  tab <- table(x$current_predicted_triage, x[[paste0(code_value, "_majority")]] == 1)
  if (nrow(tab) < 2 || ncol(tab) < 2) {
    return("unavailable")
  }
  fmt_p(suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab, correct = FALSE)$p.value))
}

question_disclosure_groups <- triage_delta_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(current_predicted_triage)) %>%
  mutate(
    acute_risk_question = if_any(all_of(c("SI", "SP", "HI", "SG", "IM")), ~ .x == 1),
    contextual_question = if_any(all_of(c("RF", "SS", "TX", "HX", "OE")), ~ .x == 1)
  )

question_disclosure_rate <- function(group_col, disclosure_col, group_value = TRUE, predicted_value = NULL) {
  x <- question_disclosure_groups
  if (!is.null(predicted_value)) {
    x <- x %>% filter(as.character(current_predicted_triage) == predicted_value)
  }
  x %>%
    filter(.data[[group_col]] == group_value) %>%
    summarise(rate = mean(.data[[disclosure_col]] > 0, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    pull(rate) %>%
    .[1]
}

question_disclosure_pred_ad_p_text <- function(group_col, disclosure_col) {
  x <- question_disclosure_groups %>%
    filter(.data[[group_col]], as.character(current_predicted_triage) %in% c("A", "D")) %>%
    mutate(current_predicted_triage = droplevels(current_predicted_triage))
  tab <- table(x$current_predicted_triage, x[[disclosure_col]] > 0)
  if (nrow(tab) < 2 || ncol(tab) < 2) {
    return("unavailable")
  }
  fmt_p(suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab, correct = FALSE)$p.value))
}

question_acute_risk_factor <- question_disclosure_rate("acute_risk_question", "n_new_risk_factor")
question_nonacute_risk_factor <- question_disclosure_rate("acute_risk_question", "n_new_risk_factor", FALSE)
question_acute_relevant_negative <- question_disclosure_rate("acute_risk_question", "n_new_relevant_negative")
question_nonacute_relevant_negative <- question_disclosure_rate("acute_risk_question", "n_new_relevant_negative", FALSE)
question_context_protective <- question_disclosure_rate("contextual_question", "n_new_protective_factor")
question_noncontext_protective <- question_disclosure_rate("contextual_question", "n_new_protective_factor", FALSE)
question_context_precip <- question_disclosure_rate("contextual_question", "n_new_precipitating_maintaining")
question_noncontext_precip <- question_disclosure_rate("contextual_question", "n_new_precipitating_maintaining", FALSE)
question_context_a_protective <- question_disclosure_rate("contextual_question", "n_new_protective_factor", TRUE, "A")
question_context_d_protective <- question_disclosure_rate("contextual_question", "n_new_protective_factor", TRUE, "D")
question_context_a_negative <- question_disclosure_rate("contextual_question", "n_new_relevant_negative", TRUE, "A")
question_context_d_negative <- question_disclosure_rate("contextual_question", "n_new_relevant_negative", TRUE, "D")
question_context_a_risk <- question_disclosure_rate("contextual_question", "n_new_risk_factor", TRUE, "A")
question_context_d_risk <- question_disclosure_rate("contextual_question", "n_new_risk_factor", TRUE, "D")
question_acute_risk_factor_p <- question_binary_p_text(question_disclosure_groups, "acute_risk_question", "n_new_risk_factor")
question_acute_negative_p <- question_binary_p_text(question_disclosure_groups, "acute_risk_question", "n_new_relevant_negative")
question_context_protective_p <- question_binary_p_text(question_disclosure_groups, "contextual_question", "n_new_protective_factor")
question_context_precip_p <- question_binary_p_text(question_disclosure_groups, "contextual_question", "n_new_precipitating_maintaining")
question_context_protective_ad_p <- question_disclosure_pred_ad_p_text("contextual_question", "n_new_protective_factor")
question_context_negative_ad_p <- question_disclosure_pred_ad_p_text("contextual_question", "n_new_relevant_negative")
question_context_risk_ad_p <- question_disclosure_pred_ad_p_text("contextual_question", "n_new_risk_factor")
question_pred_oe_ad_p <- question_pred_ad_p_text("OE")
question_pred_im_ad_p <- question_pred_ad_p_text("IM")
question_pred_ph_ad_p <- question_pred_ad_p_text("PH")
question_pred_sg_ad_p <- question_pred_ad_p_text("SG")

final_turn_metric_columns <- c(
  "Turn 1", "Turn 5", "Turn 10",
  "Change (Turn 10 - Turn 1)",
  "Turn 10 clinician clear-only"
)

final_turn_table <- bind_rows(
  final_turn_table_raw %>% mutate(Metric = "Macro accuracy"),
  final_turn_bias_raw %>% mutate(Metric = "Mean triage error")
) %>%
  mutate(
    display_model = factor(display_model, levels = final_turn_table_raw$display_model),
    Metric = factor(Metric, levels = c("Macro accuracy", "Mean triage error"))
  ) %>%
  arrange(display_model, Metric) %>%
  select(Model = display_model, Metric, all_of(final_turn_metric_columns)) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      all_of(final_turn_metric_columns),
      ~ if_else(
        Metric == "Macro accuracy",
        fmt_pct(.x),
        if_else(is.na(.x), NA_character_, sprintf("%.2f", .x))
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
```

# Abstract

People who bring mental-health concerns to AI chatbots rarely state the full clinical picture at once. Models that do well on single-turn benchmarks, where a complete case is presented in only one message, may therefore fail in deployment, where the decisive facts surface across multiple turns.

We evaluated the triage performance of `r n_models` frontier AI chatbots in `r max_turn`-turn conversations with a simulated user grounded in one of `r n_vignettes` psychiatric vignettes, spanning routine to emergency presentations. Conversational triage exposed two clinically relevant chatbot behaviors that are invisible to single-turn benchmarks. The first was a failure of inference: chatbots showed a self-consistency bias that caused early errors to persist, leading to sustained under-triage of emergencies. The second was a failure of context-seeking: chatbots that focused on risk and safety were sensitive to emergencies but over-triaged milder cases, whereas chatbots that explored the presentation more broadly stayed calibrated on milder cases but missed many emergencies. Chatbots tended to show more repetitive context-seeking than human clinicians, which was associated with more triage errors.

Our results indicate that multi-turn evaluation exposes chatbot risks that single-turn benchmarks miss, and point to inference and context-seeking behaviors as concrete targets for safer AI mental-health systems.

# Introduction

People increasingly turn to consumer AI chatbots to discuss their mental health and associated day-to-day challenges [@opel_transforming_2026; @busch_current_2025; @costa-gomes_public_2026; @noauthor_introducing_2026]. When these queries concern new or evolving mental-health problems, a chatbot’s response can function as an implicit or explicit triage recommendation: a judgment about whether a user should seek medical or psychological assessment, and how soon [@sands_development_2016; @stigter-outshoven_competencies_2024; @liu_foundational_2025; @liu_multi-agent_2026]. Recent work has begun to evaluate consumer chatbots in psychiatric triage, as well as medical triage more broadly. In psychiatry, this work has often produced a generally reassuring picture, with AI chatbots recognizing many psychiatric emergencies when sufficient information is presented in a single user query (*single-turn* triage) [@hager_evaluation_2024; @gaber_evaluating_2025; @nedos_is_2026; @ramaswamy_chatgpt_2026; @rao_large_2026; @weilnhammer_one-shot_2026].

However, single-turn evaluations sidestep a central feature of real health-related chatbot interactions: users rarely provide all clinically relevant information in one message [@weilnhammer_one-shot_2026]. Instead, they provide information across multiple turns as a function of what questions the chatbot asks, a phenomenon known as context-seeking [@dohnany_technological_2025; @weilnhammer_vulnerability-amplifying_2026; @morrin_artificial_2026; @moore_characterizing_2026]. Consequently, real-world triage errors may arise when a chatbot misinterprets triage-relevant facts that are already available (an inference failure) or, alternatively, when it fails to elicit the relevant information in the first place (a context-seeking failure) [@johri_evaluation_2025; @schmidgall_agentclinic_2026]. Models that appear safe when handed a complete case in one message may therefore fail in the multi-turn interactions that are typically seen in production [@bean_clinical_2025; @laban_llms_2025], a phenomenon known as the deployment gap [@gong_knowledge-practice_2025]. Closing this gap requires simulating the full pathway from model question, to user disclosure, to triage update across extended conversations between users and chatbots.

Here, we adapted a structured psychiatric vignette corpus [@weilnhammer_one-shot_2026] to evaluate `r n_models` target chatbots in psychiatric conversational triage. We simulated `r n_conversations` conversations with users seeking help for mental-health or neuropsychiatric concerns, each anchored to a hidden case file containing the information needed to map the case onto 1 of 4 triage dispositions, ranging from routine care, assessment within a week, urgent assessment within one or two days, to emergency-level care straight away. The case file seeded only the user model and was not revealed to the target chatbot, which had to elicit the triage-relevant information through follow-up questions while issuing an independent triage disposition at every turn [@fouda_psychiatrybench_2026; @pan_compass-gh_2026].

Our results confirm at least two deployment gaps exposed by conversational triage. The first is a failure of inference: as conversations lengthened, chatbots increasingly deferred to their own running triage disposition rather than the emerging clinical picture, leaving many emergencies persistently under-triaged. The second is a failure of context-seeking: chatbots relied on recurring lines of questioning whose direction shaped error — escalation-focused questioning caught emergencies but over-triaged milder cases, whereas broader questioning stayed calibrated on milder cases but missed more emergencies — and perseverated on a single line of questioning more than human clinicians did.

# Results

## Benchmark and simulation workflow

We investigated conversational triage on a set of `r n_vignettes` structured psychiatric cases spanning `r n_presentation_clusters` mental-health and neuropsychiatric presentations, `r n_risk_domains` risk domains, and four triage dispositions (Figure 1). Each case contained a complete hidden clinical scenario anchored on a benchmark triage label ranging from A (routine care or already scheduled follow-up), through B (assessment within 1 week) and C (urgent assessment within 24–48 hours), to D (emergency-level care now). The vignettes were generated using a clinician-in-the-loop LLM workflow and verified through independent consensus annotations from `r clinician_n_raters` clinicians (`r clinician_total_ratings` triage ratings across the `r n_vignettes` cases, `r sprintf("%.1f", clinician_mean_ratings)` per case on average) [@weilnhammer_one-shot_2026]. The same vignette set was previously used to evaluate single-turn triage performance in the `r n_models` target chatbots tested here, providing a matched performance reference against which we could test how single-turn evaluation estimated the triage performance of a given AI chatbot relative to the conversational setting [@weilnhammer_one-shot_2026].

For each user vignette, we simulated separate conversations of `r n_models` target chatbots from major model providers with a user simulator LLM model (Anthropic’s Claude Opus 4.6), instantiated with one of `r n_vignettes` clinical vignettes in the system prompt (see Methods and Figure 1). The user system prompt contained detailed instructions to only emit a single clinical fact in each conversation turn, and to only do so if asked a question about this fact by the target chatbot.

At each conversation turn, we generated two independent responses from the target chatbot. The first response was a provisional triage disposition (A, B, C, or D). The second response was a follow-up question to help discern triage acuity, with no visibility into any prior triage disposition. Only the follow-up question was passed to the simulated user, whose vignette-grounded response was appended to the growing transcript together with the target chatbot’s question. Thus, each subsequent question and each subsequent triage disposition was generated from the full preceding user–chatbot conversation, while the provisional triage dispositions themselves were not added to the conversation. This allowed us to elicit chatbot triage dispositions at each conversation turn without contaminating the main user–chatbot interaction with the presence of these judgments [@kumaran_competing_2026]. The Supplement describes the vignette design (Table S1), vignette fields (Table S2), exact prompts (Table S3), and chatbot actions at each turn of the conversation (Table S4).

```{r}
#| label: figure_1_workflow_schema
#| echo: false
#| out-width: 100%
knitr::include_graphics("workflow_overview.png")
```

**Figure 1.** Overview of the conversational psychiatric triage benchmark workflow, in three stages. Clinician-defined design axes (presentation clusters × risk domains × A–D urgency levels) and AI-generated structured vignettes, each paired with a triage-discriminative checklist, define the hidden case ground truth; clinician consensus validates the A–D labels, and `r n_vignettes` hidden cases seed the user simulator. Each of `r n_models` target chatbots holds a `r max_turn`-turn conversation with the user simulator (Claude Opus 4.6), which is anchored on the hidden case and discloses a single fact only when asked by the target chatbot. Seeing only the visible transcript, the target chatbot makes separate follow-up-question and provisional-triage API calls at every turn, producing an A–D triage label per turn that refines as facts are disclosed. Post hoc coding of every turn links question type (15 codes), newly disclosed triage-discriminative checklist items, and 6 disclosure categories to subsequent triage revisions, resolving into two failure modes: biased belief updating (Gap 1) and rigid context-seeking (Gap 2).

## Triage accuracy improves over turns but errors drift toward over-triage

We defined triage accuracy as the tendency for a chatbot to select the correct triage disposition (A, B, C or D) at each turn of a conversation, and triage error as the direction of its mistakes — positive when a chatbot over-triaged relative to the benchmark and negative when it under-triaged. Triage accuracy improved over the conversation, increasing from `r fmt_pct(llm_turn1_acc)` at turn 1 to `r fmt_pct(llm_final_acc)` at turn `r max_turn` (main effect of turn: `r turn_accuracy_lm_text`), with most of the gain occurring over the first few turns (Figure 2A; Table S5). 

As conversations unfolded, triage errors drifted upward (main effect of turn: `r turn_signed_error_lm_text`). This drift produced increasing over-triage for routine (A; triage error rising from `r sprintf("%+.2f", llm_A_turn1_signed)` at turn 1 to `r sprintf("%+.2f", llm_A_final_signed)` at turn `r max_turn`) and intermediate (B; from `r sprintf("%+.2f", llm_B_turn1_signed)` to `r sprintf("%+.2f", llm_B_final_signed)`) cases, and improved calibration for urgent (C; from `r sprintf("%+.2f", llm_C_turn1_signed)` to `r sprintf("%+.2f", llm_C_final_signed)`) and emergency cases (D; from `r sprintf("%+.2f", llm_D_turn1_signed)` to `r sprintf("%+.2f", llm_D_final_signed)`). By the final turn, conversational triage errors approached those from single-turn triage, in which chatbots received all triage-relevant information in a single message (R = `r conversation_full_disclosure_signed_R`, P `r conversation_full_disclosure_signed_P`).

## Emergency under-triage persists despite near-complete disclosure

Detecting emergencies is the most critical function of AI triage [@sax_association_2026; @sax_emergency_2025]. After `r max_turn` turns, chatbots under-triaged `r fmt_pct(1 - llm_D_final_acc)` of emergencies, compared with `r fmt_pct(1 - one_shot_D_acc)` from a single full-disclosure message (Figure 2; see Figure S1 for a replication with clinician consensus labels) [@weilnhammer_one-shot_2026]. This `r fmt_pct((1 - llm_D_final_acc) - (1 - one_shot_D_acc))` gap indicates that single-turn benchmarks [@nedos_is_2026; @ramaswamy_chatgpt_2026] may overestimate real-world performance where users typically disclose information gradually across multiple turns [@bean_clinical_2025; @laban_llms_2025].

To understand the cause of emergency under-triage in conversational settings, we constructed an LLM judge to quantify the proportion of clinically relevant information revealed by the user simulator at each conversation turn (see Methods for details). Scoring was reliable across 3 repeated scorings (Fleiss' κ = `r round(disclosure_kappa, 3)`). Disclosure increased with diminishing returns throughout the conversation, surfacing a median of `r fmt_pct(disclosure_final_median)` of each case’s triage-discriminative facts by the final turn (IQR `r fmt_pct(disclosure_final_q1)`–`r fmt_pct(disclosure_final_q3)`; Figure S2A). Only `r final_fact_never_D_n` of `r final_fact_D_n` emergency-level facts (`r fmt_pct(final_fact_never_D)`; `r fmt_pct(final_fact_never_overall)` overall) were never revealed in any conversation, confirming the simulator disclosed almost any fact when asked directly.

The gap between single- and multi-turn triage was unrelated to the difference in triage-relevant information that surfaced in the two settings: across vignettes, triage errors were unrelated to how much had been disclosed (`r disclosure_error_vignette_cor_label`; Figure S2B), and disclosure rate did not differentiate correctly identified from under-triaged emergencies (D; `r disclosure_outcome_mwu_D_label`; Figure S2C). When we compared each conversational case to its own single-turn counterpart — where disclosure was near-complete (`r fmt_pct(one_shot_disclosure_overall_mean)` of discriminative facts; Figure S2D) — the amount of missing information was uncorrelated with the difference in triage performance between the conversational and single-turn settings (`r disclosure_gap_vignette_cor_label`; Figure S2E).

Together, our results indicate that the ingredients for accurate triage were often present — simulated users revealed most triage-relevant facts when interacting with AI chatbots over `r max_turn` turns, and models recognized psychiatric urgency when presented in a single message — yet conversational triage did not reliably converge on correct final recommendations. This indicates that triage performance is modulated by the conversational process itself: how chatbots reason over information as it is revealed gradually over multiple turns (gap 1: inference), and how their own questions shape the conversation with the simulated user (gap 2: context-seeking).

```{r}
#| label: figure2_primary_data
#| include: false
confusion_by_model_turn <- llm_turn_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(predicted_triage), !is.na(gold_triage)) %>%
  count(display_model, turn, gold_triage, predicted_triage, name = "n") %>%
  complete(
    display_model,
    turn = seq_len(max_turn),
    gold_triage = factor(triage_levels, levels = triage_levels),
    predicted_triage = factor(triage_levels, levels = triage_levels),
    fill = list(n = 0L)
  ) %>%
  group_by(display_model, turn, gold_triage) %>%
  mutate(prop = if (sum(n) > 0) n / sum(n) else NA_real_) %>%
  ungroup
confusion_average_turn <- confusion_by_model_turn %>%
  group_by(turn, gold_triage, predicted_triage) %>%
  summarise(prop = mean(prop, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    turn_label = factor(paste0("Turn ", turn), levels = paste0("Turn ", seq_len(max_turn))),
    cell_direction = purrr::map2_chr(gold_triage, predicted_triage, classify_confusion_cell),
    fill_color = purrr::map2_chr(
      cell_direction,
      prop,
      ~ blend_to_white(triage_direction_palette[[.x]], pmin(pmax(.y, 0), 1))
    ),
    text_color = contrast_text_color(fill_color)
  )

fig2a <- ggplot(confusion_average_turn, aes(x = predicted_triage, y = gold_triage, fill = fill_color)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.0f", 100 * prop), color = text_color), size = heatmap_label_size) +
  facet_wrap(~ turn_label, nrow = 1) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(triage_levels)) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  scale_color_identity(guide = "none") +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(x = "Predicted triage", y = "Benchmark triage", title = "A", subtitle = "Errors drift from under- toward over-triage across turns") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

figure2_turn_alpha_values <- scales::rescale(seq_len(max_turn), to = c(0.08, 1), from = c(1, max_turn))
names(figure2_turn_alpha_values) <- paste0("Turn ", seq_len(max_turn))

figure2_disclosure_accuracy_turn_model <- disclosure_accuracy_turn_model %>%
  mutate(
    turn_alpha = unname(figure2_turn_alpha_values[as.character(turn_factor)]),
    point_fill = scales::alpha(model_palette[as.character(display_model)], turn_alpha)
  )

figure2_disclosure_accuracy_turn_average <- disclosure_accuracy_turn_average %>%
  mutate(
    turn_alpha = unname(figure2_turn_alpha_values[as.character(turn_factor)]),
    point_fill = scales::alpha("black", turn_alpha)
  )

plot_figure2_disclosure_metric <- function(metric, y_label, title, subtitle = NULL, y_limits = NULL, y_breaks = waiver(), y_percent = FALSE) {
  if (is.null(y_limits)) {
    y_limits <- metric_limits(
      c(figure2_disclosure_accuracy_turn_model[[metric]], figure2_disclosure_accuracy_turn_average[[metric]]),
      include_zero = TRUE
    )
  }
  reference_layer <- list()
  if (metric == "signed_error") {
    reference_layer <- c(reference_layer, list(
      geom_hline(
        yintercept = 0,
        linetype = "dashed",
        color = "grey45",
        linewidth = 0.32
      ),
      geom_hline(
        data = full_vignette_by_gold_average,
        aes(yintercept = .data[[metric]]),
        linetype = "dotted",
        color = "grey45",
        linewidth = 0.4
      ),
      geom_vline(
        data = disclosure_final_by_original_level,
        aes(xintercept = mean_disclosure),
        linetype = "dashed",
        color = "black",
        linewidth = 0.4
      ),
      geom_vline(
        data = one_shot_disclosure_by_gold,
        aes(xintercept = mean_disclosure),
        linetype = "dotted",
        color = "grey45",
        linewidth = 0.4
      ),
      geom_text(
        data = dplyr::filter(figure2_disclosure_vline_labels, label == "Conversation"),
        aes(x = x, y = -1.9, label = label),
        inherit.aes = FALSE,
        nudge_x = -0.05,
        angle = 90, hjust = 0, vjust = 1.25,
        size = base_font_size * 0.20,
        color = "black"
      ),
      geom_text(
        data = dplyr::filter(figure2_disclosure_vline_labels, label == "single-turn ceiling"),
        aes(x = x, y = -1.9, label = label),
        inherit.aes = FALSE,
        nudge_x = -0.05,
        angle = 90, hjust = 0, vjust = 1.25,
        size = base_font_size * 0.20,
        color = "grey35"
      )
    ))
  } else if (metric == "accuracy") {
    reference_layer <- c(reference_layer, list(
      geom_hline(
        data = full_vignette_by_gold_average,
        aes(yintercept = .data[[metric]]),
        linetype = "dashed",
        color = "grey45",
        linewidth = 0.32
      )
    ))
  }
  ggplot(
    figure2_disclosure_accuracy_turn_model,
    aes(x = mean_disclosure, y = .data[[metric]], color = display_model, group = display_model)
  ) +
    reference_layer +
    geom_path(linewidth = 0.42, alpha = 0.45, lineend = "round") +
    geom_point(
      aes(fill = point_fill),
      shape = 21,
      size = manuscript_point_size * 0.90,
      stroke = 0.65,
      color = "black"
    ) +
    geom_path(
      data = figure2_disclosure_accuracy_turn_average,
      aes(x = mean_disclosure, y = .data[[metric]], group = gold_triage),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 1.05,
      alpha = 0.92,
      lineend = "round"
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = figure2_disclosure_accuracy_turn_average,
      aes(x = mean_disclosure, y = .data[[metric]], fill = point_fill),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      shape = 21,
      color = "black",
      size = manuscript_point_size * 0.90,
      stroke = 0.75
    ) +
    facet_grid(. ~ gold_triage, labeller = as_labeller(triage_labels_short)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = model_palette, name = NULL, drop = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_identity() +
    scale_x_continuous(
      labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
      limits = c(0, 1),
      breaks = c(0, 0.5, 1)
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      labels = if (y_percent) label_percent(accuracy = 1) else waiver(),
      limits = y_limits,
      breaks = y_breaks
    ) +
    labs(
      x = "Discriminative features disclosed",
      y = y_label,
      title = title,
      subtitle = subtitle
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = base_font_size * 1.02)
    ) +
    guides(color = "none", fill = "none")
}

fig2b_signed_disclosure <- plot_figure2_disclosure_metric(
  "signed_error",
  "Mean triage error",
  "B",
  "Disclosure raises urgency, over-shooting low-acuity cases",
  y_limits = c(-2, 1),
  y_breaks = -2:1,
  y_percent = FALSE
)

figure2_disclosure_model_legend_plot <- ggplot(
  figure2_disclosure_accuracy_turn_model %>% distinct(display_model, mean_disclosure, accuracy),
  aes(x = mean_disclosure, y = accuracy, color = display_model)
) +
  geom_line(aes(group = display_model), linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_palette, name = NULL, drop = FALSE) +
  theme_void(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = base_font_size * 0.62),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.24, "cm"),
    legend.key.width = unit(0.42, "cm")
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE))

fig2_model_legend <- extract_legend(figure2_disclosure_model_legend_plot)

fig2c_disclosure_outcome <- ggplot(
  disclosure_final_by_outcome_model,
  aes(x = triage_outcome, y = mean_disclosure)
) +
  geom_point(
    aes(fill = display_model),
    shape = 21,
    color = "black",
    size = manuscript_point_size * 0.90,
    stroke = 0.65,
    alpha = 0.55,
    position = position_jitter(width = 0.10, height = 0)
  ) +
  geom_linerange(
    data = disclosure_final_by_outcome_summary,
    aes(x = triage_outcome, ymin = lower, ymax = upper),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.75
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = disclosure_final_by_outcome_summary,
    aes(x = triage_outcome, y = mean),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "black",
    size = manuscript_point_size
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = disclosure_outcome_test_by_level %>%
      mutate(
        label = ifelse(is.na(test_p), "n/a", paste0("P ", vapply(test_p, fmt_p, character(1)))),
        gold_triage = factor(as.character(gold_triage), levels = triage_levels)
      ),
    aes(x = 1, y = Inf, label = label),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1.5,
    size = base_font_size * 0.24
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~gold_triage, nrow = 1, scales = "free_x", labeller = as_labeller(triage_labels_short)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = model_palette, name = NULL, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Final-turn disclosure",
    title = "C",
    subtitle = "Final-turn disclosure by triage outcome"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1, size = base_font_size * 0.68),
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_font_size * 0.76),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

primary_metric_model <- llm_by_gold_turn %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(accuracy, signed_error, absolute_error),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  )

primary_metric_average <- primary_metric_model %>%
  group_by(gold_triage, turn, metric) %>%
  summarise(value = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

primary_metric_limits <- primary_metric_model %>%
  bind_rows(primary_metric_average) %>%
  group_by(metric) %>%
  summarise(
    lower = case_when(
      first(metric) == "accuracy" ~ metric_limits(value, lower_bound = 0, upper_bound = 1)[1],
      first(metric) == "signed_error" ~ metric_limits(value, lower_bound = -3, upper_bound = 3, include_zero = TRUE)[1],
      first(metric) == "absolute_error" ~ metric_limits(value, lower_bound = 0, upper_bound = 3, include_zero = TRUE)[1]
    ),
    upper = case_when(
      first(metric) == "accuracy" ~ metric_limits(value, lower_bound = 0, upper_bound = 1)[2],
      first(metric) == "signed_error" ~ metric_limits(value, lower_bound = -3, upper_bound = 3, include_zero = TRUE)[2],
      first(metric) == "absolute_error" ~ metric_limits(value, lower_bound = 0, upper_bound = 3, include_zero = TRUE)[2]
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

get_metric_limits <- function(metric_name) {
  limits <- primary_metric_limits %>% filter(metric == metric_name)
  c(limits$lower[1], limits$upper[1])
}

plot_primary_metric <- function(metric_name, title, subtitle, y_label, percent = FALSE, show_zero = FALSE, show_legend = FALSE) {
  df_model <- primary_metric_model %>% filter(metric == metric_name, is.finite(value))
  df_avg <- primary_metric_average %>% filter(metric == metric_name, is.finite(value))
  y_limits <- get_metric_limits(metric_name)
  y_breaks <- metric_breaks(y_limits, percent = percent)
  y_scale <- if (percent) {
    scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1), limits = y_limits, breaks = y_breaks)
  } else {
    scale_y_continuous(limits = y_limits, breaks = y_breaks)
  }
  model_guide <- if (show_legend) {
    guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE, override.aes = list(linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 1, size = manuscript_point_size * 0.85))
  } else {
    "none"
  }

  p <- ggplot()
  if (metric_name == "accuracy") {
    p <- p +
      geom_hline(
        data = full_vignette_by_gold_average,
        aes(yintercept = accuracy),
        linetype = "dashed",
        color = "grey45",
        linewidth = 0.32
      )
  }
  if (metric_name == "signed_error") {
    p <- p +
      geom_hline(
        data = full_vignette_by_gold_average,
        aes(yintercept = signed_error),
        linetype = "dashed",
        color = "grey45",
        linewidth = 0.32
      )
  }
  p <- p +
    geom_line(
      data = df_model,
      aes(x = turn, y = value, color = display_model, group = display_model),
      linewidth = 0.35,
      alpha = 0.55
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = df_model,
      aes(x = turn, y = value, fill = display_model),
      shape = 21,
      color = "black",
      size = manuscript_point_size * 0.90,
      stroke = 0.65,
      alpha = 0.65
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = df_avg,
      aes(x = turn, y = value, group = 1),
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 0.95
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = df_avg,
      aes(x = turn, y = value),
      color = "black",
      size = manuscript_point_size + 0.2
    ) +
    facet_grid(. ~ gold_triage, labeller = as_labeller(triage_labels_short)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = model_palette, name = "Target chatbot", guide = model_guide) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = model_palette, name = "Target chatbot", guide = "none") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq_len(max_turn)) +
    y_scale +
    labs(x = "Turn", y = y_label, title = title, subtitle = subtitle) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = base_font_size * 0.95),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.text = element_text(size = base_font_size * 0.68),
      legend.key.width = grid::unit(0.75, "lines"),
      legend.key.height = grid::unit(0.45, "lines"),
      legend.spacing.x = grid::unit(0.08, "lines"),
      legend.spacing.y = grid::unit(0.02, "lines")
    )
  if (show_zero) {
    p <- p + geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3)
  }
  p
}

```

```{r}
#| label: figure_2_conversational_performance
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 7
#| out-width: 100%
gridExtra::grid.arrange(
  fig2a,
  fig2b_signed_disclosure,
  fig2_model_legend,
  ncol = 1,
  heights = c(0.5, 1, 0.12)
)
```

**Figure 2.** Conversational triage performance approaches the single-turn benchmark. **A.** Average confusion matrices across target chatbots at each conversational turn (turns 1–10, left-to-right); each row sums to 100%. Blue cells indicate correct predictions, green over-triage, and red under-triage. **B.** Triage error as triage-discriminative information is disclosed over the course of a conversation, shown separately for benchmark levels A–D. Colored lines represent individual chatbots and black lines their average; lighter points are earlier turns. Positive values indicate over-triage and negative values under-triage. The dashed horizontal line at zero marks perfect calibration, and the dotted horizontal line shows the single-turn, full-disclosure benchmark for the same cases. Vertical lines mark the mean fraction of discriminative features disclosed by the final conversational turn (black dashed line) and present in the single-turn full-disclosure message (grey dotted line).

```{r}
#| label: figure_anchoring_data
#| include: false

# Belief-updating / anchoring model. The current triage disposition is
# modeled as a weighted combination of the model's own preceding triage disposition (anchor),
# the same model's single-turn full-disclosure disposition for the case (own-judgment pull),
# and counts of newly disclosed features in each of the six disclosure categories,
# with every effect allowed to vary across conversation turns, model-specific fixed
# intercepts, and a vignette random intercept. Triage levels coded A-D as 0-3.

anchoring_fact_terms <- c(
  "n_new_risk_factor",
  "n_new_clinical_symptom",
  "n_new_precipitating_maintaining",
  "n_new_predisposing",
  "n_new_relevant_negative",
  "n_new_protective_factor"
)

anchoring_fact_labels <- c(
  "n_new_risk_factor" = "Risk factor",
  "n_new_clinical_symptom" = "Clinical symptom",
  "n_new_precipitating_maintaining" = "Precipitating / maintaining factor",
  "n_new_predisposing" = "Predisposing factor",
  "n_new_relevant_negative" = "Relevant negative",
  "n_new_protective_factor" = "Protective factor"
)

# Each chatbot's own single-turn, full-disclosure triage disposition per vignette, on the
# same A-D = 0-3 ordinal scale. This is the model's best single-message judgment
# of the case, given all the facts at once, and serves as the comparison anchor in
# the belief-updating model (in place of benchmark ground truth): we ask how much
# each running disposition is pulled toward the model's own full-information
# conclusion versus its preceding-turn disposition. Joined on display model so that
# conversational model_short labels match the single-turn target_model_label space.
anchoring_oneshot_lookup <- full_vignette_one_shot_df %>%
  transmute(
    display_model = as.character(display_model),
    vignette_id,
    oneshot_ordinal = as.integer(factor(as.character(predicted_triage), levels = triage_levels)) - 1
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(oneshot_ordinal)) %>%
  distinct(display_model, vignette_id, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Full conversations spanning transitions 1->2 ... 9->10, with trajectories and
# disclosure counts. See
# scripts/triage_delta_regression/build_anchoring_turn10_dataframe.py.
anchoring_model_df <- readr::read_csv(
  "../analysis/triage_delta_regression/data/anchoring_turn10_dataframe.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(predicted_ordinal_t),
    !is.na(predicted_ordinal_t1),
    !is.na(gold_ordinal),
    !is.na(turn)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    display_model = as.character(display_model(as.character(model_short))),
    predicted_ordinal_t = as.numeric(predicted_ordinal_t),
    predicted_ordinal_t1 = as.numeric(predicted_ordinal_t1),
    gold_ordinal = as.numeric(gold_ordinal),
    turn = as.numeric(turn),
    model_short = factor(model_short)
  ) %>%
  inner_join(anchoring_oneshot_lookup, by = c("display_model", "vignette_id")) %>%
  mutate(oneshot_ordinal = as.numeric(oneshot_ordinal))

anchoring_formula <- as.formula(paste(
  "predicted_ordinal_t1 ~ model_short +",
  "predicted_ordinal_t * turn + oneshot_ordinal * turn +",
  paste(sprintf("%s * turn", anchoring_fact_terms), collapse = " + "),
  "+ (1 | vignette_id)"
))

anchoring_fit <- lmerTest::lmer(anchoring_formula, data = anchoring_model_df, REML = TRUE)
anchoring_coef <- as.data.frame(summary(anchoring_fit)$coefficients)
anchoring_coef$term <- rownames(anchoring_coef)
names(anchoring_coef)[names(anchoring_coef) == "Estimate"] <- "estimate"
names(anchoring_coef)[names(anchoring_coef) == "Std. Error"] <- "std_error"
names(anchoring_coef)[names(anchoring_coef) == "Pr(>|t|)"] <- "p_value"
anchoring_vcov <- as.matrix(vcov(anchoring_fit))

# Resolve interaction term names regardless of the order R stores them in.
anchoring_resolve <- function(term) {
  if (term %in% anchoring_coef$term) {
    return(term)
  }
  if (grepl(":", term)) {
    alt <- paste(rev(strsplit(term, ":", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]), collapse = ":")
    if (alt %in% anchoring_coef$term) {
      return(alt)
    }
  }
  term
}
anchoring_get <- function(term) {
  term <- anchoring_resolve(term)
  v <- anchoring_coef$estimate[anchoring_coef$term == term]
  if (length(v) == 0) 0 else v[1]
}
anchoring_get_se <- function(term) {
  term <- anchoring_resolve(term)
  v <- anchoring_coef$std_error[anchoring_coef$term == term]
  if (length(v) == 0) NA_real_ else v[1]
}
anchoring_get_p <- function(term) {
  term <- anchoring_resolve(term)
  v <- anchoring_coef$p_value[anchoring_coef$term == term]
  if (length(v) == 0) NA_real_ else v[1]
}

anchoring_turns <- sort(unique(anchoring_model_df$turn))
anchoring_mean_turn <- mean(anchoring_model_df$turn)
anchoring_mean_gold <- mean(anchoring_model_df$gold_ordinal)
anchoring_mean_oneshot <- mean(anchoring_model_df$oneshot_ordinal)

# weight(turn) = b0 + b1 * turn, with SE from the fixed-effect covariance matrix.
anchoring_weight_by_turn <- function(b0_term, b1_term, weight_label) {
  b0_term <- anchoring_resolve(b0_term)
  b1_term <- anchoring_resolve(b1_term)
  b0 <- anchoring_get(b0_term)
  b1 <- anchoring_get(b1_term)
  v00 <- anchoring_vcov[b0_term, b0_term]
  v11 <- anchoring_vcov[b1_term, b1_term]
  v01 <- anchoring_vcov[b0_term, b1_term]
  tibble(
    turn = anchoring_turns,
    weight = b0 + b1 * anchoring_turns,
    se = sqrt(pmax(v00 + anchoring_turns^2 * v11 + 2 * anchoring_turns * v01, 0)),
    component = weight_label
  )
}

anchoring_weights_df <- bind_rows(
  anchoring_weight_by_turn("predicted_ordinal_t", "predicted_ordinal_t:turn", "Own running estimate"),
  anchoring_weight_by_turn("oneshot_ordinal", "oneshot_ordinal:turn", "Own single-turn judgment")
) %>%
  mutate(
    component = factor(component, levels = c("Own running estimate", "Own single-turn judgment")),
    lower = weight - 1.96 * se,
    upper = weight + 1.96 * se
  )

anchoring_component_palette <- c(
  "Own running estimate" = "#6D28D9",
  "Own single-turn judgment" = "#0F766E"
)

# Effective weights at the mean turn (for inline reporting).
anchoring_wa_meanturn <- anchoring_get("predicted_ordinal_t") + anchoring_get("predicted_ordinal_t:turn") * anchoring_mean_turn
anchoring_wos_meanturn <- anchoring_get("oneshot_ordinal") + anchoring_get("oneshot_ordinal:turn") * anchoring_mean_turn

# Weights at the first and last conversation turn (for inline reporting).
anchoring_first_turn <- min(anchoring_turns)
anchoring_last_turn <- max(anchoring_turns)
anchoring_wa_first <- anchoring_get("predicted_ordinal_t") + anchoring_get("predicted_ordinal_t:turn") * anchoring_first_turn
anchoring_wos_first <- anchoring_get("oneshot_ordinal") + anchoring_get("oneshot_ordinal:turn") * anchoring_first_turn
anchoring_wa_last <- anchoring_get("predicted_ordinal_t") + anchoring_get("predicted_ordinal_t:turn") * anchoring_last_turn
anchoring_wos_last <- anchoring_get("oneshot_ordinal") + anchoring_get("oneshot_ordinal:turn") * anchoring_last_turn

# Empirical (model-free) agreement fractions for panel A. For each chatbot and
# turn, the fraction of next-turn labels that exactly repeat the previous-turn
# label (persistence on the running estimate) and that exactly match the chatbot's
# own single-turn, full-disclosure label. Plotted against each other, one point per
# chatbot-turn, with turn encoded by opacity.
anchoring_empirical_3a <- anchoring_model_df %>%
  group_by(display_model, turn) %>%
  summarise(
    frac_match_prev = mean(predicted_ordinal_t1 == predicted_ordinal_t),
    frac_match_oneshot = mean(predicted_ordinal_t1 == oneshot_ordinal),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(display_model = factor(display_model, levels = names(model_palette)))

# Encode turn by fading the model-colored fill, exactly as in Figure 2.
anchoring_turn_alpha_values <- scales::rescale(anchoring_turns, to = c(0.08, 1), from = range(anchoring_turns))
names(anchoring_turn_alpha_values) <- as.character(anchoring_turns)

anchoring_empirical_3a <- anchoring_empirical_3a %>%
  mutate(
    turn_alpha = unname(anchoring_turn_alpha_values[as.character(turn)]),
    point_fill = scales::alpha(model_palette[as.character(display_model)], turn_alpha)
  )

# Across-model average trajectory (black line/points, faded by turn).
anchoring_empirical_3a_avg <- anchoring_model_df %>%
  group_by(turn) %>%
  summarise(
    frac_match_prev = mean(predicted_ordinal_t1 == predicted_ordinal_t),
    frac_match_oneshot = mean(predicted_ordinal_t1 == oneshot_ordinal),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    turn_alpha = unname(anchoring_turn_alpha_values[as.character(turn)]),
    point_fill = scales::alpha("black", turn_alpha)
  )

anchoring_emp_by_turn <- anchoring_model_df %>%
  group_by(turn) %>%
  summarise(
    persist = mean(predicted_ordinal_t1 == predicted_ordinal_t),
    oneshot_match = mean(predicted_ordinal_t1 == oneshot_ordinal),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
anchoring_persist_first <- anchoring_emp_by_turn$persist[anchoring_emp_by_turn$turn == anchoring_first_turn]
anchoring_persist_last <- anchoring_emp_by_turn$persist[anchoring_emp_by_turn$turn == anchoring_last_turn]
anchoring_oneshot_match_first <- anchoring_emp_by_turn$oneshot_match[anchoring_emp_by_turn$turn == anchoring_first_turn]
anchoring_oneshot_match_last <- anchoring_emp_by_turn$oneshot_match[anchoring_emp_by_turn$turn == anchoring_last_turn]

# Fact-category main effects on the next estimate.
anchoring_fact_df <- tibble(term = anchoring_fact_terms) %>%
  mutate(
    estimate = map_dbl(term, anchoring_get),
    std_error = map_dbl(term, anchoring_get_se),
    p_value = map_dbl(term, anchoring_get_p),
    lower = estimate - 1.96 * std_error,
    upper = estimate + 1.96 * std_error,
    label = unname(anchoring_fact_labels[term]),
    direction = case_when(
      is.na(p_value) | p_value >= 0.05 ~ "Not significant",
      estimate > 0 ~ "Raises urgency",
      TRUE ~ "Lowers urgency"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(estimate) %>%
  mutate(label = factor(label, levels = label))

anchoring_fact_direction_palette <- c(
  "Raises urgency" = "#D32F2F",
  "Lowers urgency" = "#388E3C",
  "Not significant" = "#9CA3AF"
)

# Per-model steady-state signed error e* (over-triage attractor): fixed point of
# the implied first-order dynamics, at the mean turn and the model's mean single-turn
# judgment with no new disclosure. Expressed relative to benchmark ground truth so
# that e* > 0 still reads as net over-triage versus the true urgency level.
anchoring_wa_eff <- anchoring_wa_meanturn
anchoring_wos_eff <- anchoring_wos_meanturn
anchoring_turn_main_at_mean <- anchoring_get("turn") * anchoring_mean_turn
anchoring_intercept <- anchoring_get("(Intercept)")
anchoring_models <- levels(anchoring_model_df$model_short)

anchoring_attractor_df <- tibble(model_short = anchoring_models) %>%
  mutate(
    dummy = map_dbl(model_short, ~ anchoring_get(paste0("model_short", .x))),
    alpha_bias = anchoring_intercept + dummy + anchoring_turn_main_at_mean,
    L_star = (alpha_bias + anchoring_wos_eff * anchoring_mean_oneshot) / (1 - anchoring_wa_eff),
    e_star = L_star - anchoring_mean_gold,
    display_model = display_model(as.character(model_short))
  ) %>%
  arrange(e_star) %>%
  mutate(display_model = factor(display_model, levels = display_model))

# Inline scalars.
anchoring_n_obs <- nrow(anchoring_model_df)
anchoring_n_sims <- n_distinct(anchoring_model_df$sim_id)
anchoring_n_models <- n_distinct(anchoring_model_df$model_short)
anchoring_n_vignettes <- n_distinct(anchoring_model_df$vignette_id)
anchoring_anchor_turn_coef <- anchoring_get("predicted_ordinal_t:turn")
anchoring_anchor_turn_p <- anchoring_get_p("predicted_ordinal_t:turn")
anchoring_oneshot_turn_coef <- anchoring_get("oneshot_ordinal:turn")
anchoring_oneshot_turn_p <- anchoring_get_p("oneshot_ordinal:turn")
anchoring_n_overtriage_models <- sum(anchoring_attractor_df$e_star > 0)
anchoring_emax <- anchoring_attractor_df %>% slice_max(e_star, n = 1)
anchoring_emin <- anchoring_attractor_df %>% slice_min(e_star, n = 1)
anchoring_risk_coef <- anchoring_get("n_new_risk_factor")
anchoring_risk_p <- anchoring_get_p("n_new_risk_factor")
anchoring_symptom_coef <- anchoring_get("n_new_clinical_symptom")
anchoring_symptom_p <- anchoring_get_p("n_new_clinical_symptom")
anchoring_anchor_main_coef <- anchoring_get("predicted_ordinal_t")
anchoring_anchor_main_p <- anchoring_get_p("predicted_ordinal_t")
anchoring_oneshot_main_coef <- anchoring_get("oneshot_ordinal")
anchoring_oneshot_main_p <- anchoring_get_p("oneshot_ordinal")
anchoring_protect_coef <- anchoring_get("n_new_protective_factor")
anchoring_protect_p <- anchoring_get_p("n_new_protective_factor")
anchoring_relneg_coef <- anchoring_get("n_new_relevant_negative")
anchoring_relneg_p <- anchoring_get_p("n_new_relevant_negative")
```

```{r}
#| label: figure_anchoring_gold_data
#| include: false

# Ground-truth replication of the belief-updating model (Figure S4). Identical to
# the Figure 3 model in every respect, except predictor (iv) — the chatbot's own
# single-turn, full-disclosure disposition (oneshot_ordinal) — is replaced by the
# benchmark triage label (gold_ordinal). Same data, same six disclosure categories,
# same turn interactions, same per-chatbot fixed intercepts and vignette random
# intercept. This models each running disposition against the true urgency level
# rather than the chatbot's own full-information judgment.

anchoring_gold_formula <- as.formula(paste(
  "predicted_ordinal_t1 ~ model_short +",
  "predicted_ordinal_t * turn + gold_ordinal * turn +",
  paste(sprintf("%s * turn", anchoring_fact_terms), collapse = " + "),
  "+ (1 | vignette_id)"
))

anchoring_gold_fit <- lmerTest::lmer(anchoring_gold_formula, data = anchoring_model_df, REML = TRUE)
anchoring_gold_coef <- as.data.frame(summary(anchoring_gold_fit)$coefficients)
anchoring_gold_coef$term <- rownames(anchoring_gold_coef)
names(anchoring_gold_coef)[names(anchoring_gold_coef) == "Estimate"] <- "estimate"
names(anchoring_gold_coef)[names(anchoring_gold_coef) == "Std. Error"] <- "std_error"
names(anchoring_gold_coef)[names(anchoring_gold_coef) == "Pr(>|t|)"] <- "p_value"

anchoring_gold_resolve <- function(term) {
  if (term %in% anchoring_gold_coef$term) {
    return(term)
  }
  if (grepl(":", term)) {
    alt <- paste(rev(strsplit(term, ":", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]), collapse = ":")
    if (alt %in% anchoring_gold_coef$term) {
      return(alt)
    }
  }
  term
}
anchoring_gold_get <- function(term) {
  term <- anchoring_gold_resolve(term)
  v <- anchoring_gold_coef$estimate[anchoring_gold_coef$term == term]
  if (length(v) == 0) 0 else v[1]
}
anchoring_gold_get_se <- function(term) {
  term <- anchoring_gold_resolve(term)
  v <- anchoring_gold_coef$std_error[anchoring_gold_coef$term == term]
  if (length(v) == 0) NA_real_ else v[1]
}
anchoring_gold_get_p <- function(term) {
  term <- anchoring_gold_resolve(term)
  v <- anchoring_gold_coef$p_value[anchoring_gold_coef$term == term]
  if (length(v) == 0) NA_real_ else v[1]
}

# Effective weights at the mean turn.
anchoring_gold_wa_meanturn <- anchoring_gold_get("predicted_ordinal_t") + anchoring_gold_get("predicted_ordinal_t:turn") * anchoring_mean_turn
anchoring_gold_wgold_meanturn <- anchoring_gold_get("gold_ordinal") + anchoring_gold_get("gold_ordinal:turn") * anchoring_mean_turn

# Panel C: empirical agreement fractions. For each chatbot and turn, the fraction
# of next-turn dispositions that exactly repeat the previous-turn disposition
# (persistence on the running estimate) and that exactly match the benchmark label.
anchoring_gold_empirical_3a <- anchoring_model_df %>%
  group_by(display_model, turn) %>%
  summarise(
    frac_match_prev = mean(predicted_ordinal_t1 == predicted_ordinal_t),
    frac_match_gold = mean(predicted_ordinal_t1 == gold_ordinal),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    display_model = factor(display_model, levels = names(model_palette)),
    turn_alpha = unname(anchoring_turn_alpha_values[as.character(turn)]),
    point_fill = scales::alpha(model_palette[as.character(display_model)], turn_alpha)
  )

anchoring_gold_empirical_3a_avg <- anchoring_model_df %>%
  group_by(turn) %>%
  summarise(
    frac_match_prev = mean(predicted_ordinal_t1 == predicted_ordinal_t),
    frac_match_gold = mean(predicted_ordinal_t1 == gold_ordinal),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    turn_alpha = unname(anchoring_turn_alpha_values[as.character(turn)]),
    point_fill = scales::alpha("black", turn_alpha)
  )

# Panel A: fact-category main effects on the current disposition.
anchoring_gold_fact_df <- tibble(term = anchoring_fact_terms) %>%
  mutate(
    estimate = map_dbl(term, anchoring_gold_get),
    std_error = map_dbl(term, anchoring_gold_get_se),
    p_value = map_dbl(term, anchoring_gold_get_p),
    lower = estimate - 1.96 * std_error,
    upper = estimate + 1.96 * std_error,
    label = unname(anchoring_fact_labels[term]),
    direction = case_when(
      is.na(p_value) | p_value >= 0.05 ~ "Not significant",
      estimate > 0 ~ "Raises urgency",
      TRUE ~ "Lowers urgency"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(estimate) %>%
  mutate(label = factor(label, levels = label))

# Panel B: per-model steady-state signed error e* (over-triage attractor), now with
# the pull toward the benchmark label in place of the single-turn judgment.
anchoring_gold_turn_main_at_mean <- anchoring_gold_get("turn") * anchoring_mean_turn
anchoring_gold_intercept <- anchoring_gold_get("(Intercept)")
anchoring_gold_attractor_df <- tibble(model_short = anchoring_models) %>%
  mutate(
    dummy = map_dbl(model_short, ~ anchoring_gold_get(paste0("model_short", .x))),
    alpha_bias = anchoring_gold_intercept + dummy + anchoring_gold_turn_main_at_mean,
    L_star = (alpha_bias + anchoring_gold_wgold_meanturn * anchoring_mean_gold) / (1 - anchoring_gold_wa_meanturn),
    e_star = L_star - anchoring_mean_gold,
    display_model = display_model(as.character(model_short))
  ) %>%
  arrange(e_star) %>%
  mutate(display_model = factor(display_model, levels = display_model))

# Inline scalars.
anchoring_gold_anchor_main_coef <- anchoring_gold_get("predicted_ordinal_t")
anchoring_gold_anchor_main_p <- anchoring_gold_get_p("predicted_ordinal_t")
anchoring_gold_gold_main_coef <- anchoring_gold_get("gold_ordinal")
anchoring_gold_gold_main_p <- anchoring_gold_get_p("gold_ordinal")
anchoring_gold_anchor_turn_coef <- anchoring_gold_get("predicted_ordinal_t:turn")
anchoring_gold_anchor_turn_p <- anchoring_gold_get_p("predicted_ordinal_t:turn")
anchoring_gold_gold_turn_coef <- anchoring_gold_get("gold_ordinal:turn")
anchoring_gold_gold_turn_p <- anchoring_gold_get_p("gold_ordinal:turn")
anchoring_gold_risk_coef <- anchoring_gold_get("n_new_risk_factor")
anchoring_gold_risk_p <- anchoring_gold_get_p("n_new_risk_factor")
anchoring_gold_symptom_coef <- anchoring_gold_get("n_new_clinical_symptom")
anchoring_gold_symptom_p <- anchoring_gold_get_p("n_new_clinical_symptom")
anchoring_gold_protect_coef <- anchoring_gold_get("n_new_protective_factor")
anchoring_gold_protect_p <- anchoring_gold_get_p("n_new_protective_factor")
anchoring_gold_relneg_coef <- anchoring_gold_get("n_new_relevant_negative")
anchoring_gold_relneg_p <- anchoring_gold_get_p("n_new_relevant_negative")
anchoring_gold_n_overtriage_models <- sum(anchoring_gold_attractor_df$e_star > 0)
```

```{r}
#| label: error_correction_data
#| include: false

# Error-correction test of the self-consistency ("weight toward the past") effect.
# Among transitions where the chatbot's running disposition at turn t is already WRONG,
# how likely is it to be CORRECTED at t+1, and does that probability fall as the
# conversation lengthens? A declining correction rate is the accuracy signature of
# anchoring on the past: once wrong, increasingly unlikely to be updated. Uses the
# same ten-turn anchoring dataframe (predicted_ordinal_t = disposition held when asking
# at turn t; predicted_ordinal_t1 = disposition at t+1; gold_ordinal = benchmark).

error_correction_df <- anchoring_model_df %>%
  mutate(
    signed_error_t = predicted_ordinal_t - gold_ordinal,
    abs_error_t = abs(signed_error_t),
    wrong_t = abs_error_t > 0,
    under_t = signed_error_t < 0,
    over_t = signed_error_t > 0,
    emergency_under_t = gold_ordinal == 3 & predicted_ordinal_t < 3,
    corrected_t1 = as.integer(predicted_ordinal_t1 == gold_ordinal),
    gold_triage = factor(triage_levels[gold_ordinal + 1], levels = triage_levels),
    n_new_total = n_new_risk_factor + n_new_clinical_symptom +
      n_new_precipitating_maintaining + n_new_predisposing +
      n_new_relevant_negative + n_new_protective_factor
  )

# Benchmark triage-level palette, used by the Figure S3 error-correction
# panel and by the later question-phenotyping figures.
triage_assumption_palette <- c(
  "A" = "#14532D",
  "B" = "#F59E0B",
  "C" = "#F87171",
  "D" = "#991B1B"
)

# Correction-rate curves. Among transitions where the running disposition at
# turn t was already wrong, the fraction corrected to the benchmark label at t+1,
# summarised (1) by benchmark triage level (Figure S3), (2) pooled across
# all wrong transitions as a black average line, and (3) per chatbot (Figure S7F).
error_correction_by_level <- error_correction_df %>%
  filter(wrong_t) %>%
  group_by(gold_triage, turn) %>%
  summarise(n = dplyr::n(), corrected = mean(corrected_t1), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    se = sqrt(pmax(corrected * (1 - corrected), 0) / pmax(n, 1)),
    lower = pmax(corrected - 1.96 * se, 0),
    upper = pmin(corrected + 1.96 * se, 1)
  )

error_correction_overall <- error_correction_df %>%
  filter(wrong_t) %>%
  group_by(turn) %>%
  summarise(n = dplyr::n(), corrected = mean(corrected_t1), .groups = "drop")

error_correction_by_model <- error_correction_df %>%
  filter(wrong_t) %>%
  mutate(display_model = factor(display_model, levels = names(model_palette))) %>%
  group_by(display_model, turn) %>%
  summarise(n = dplyr::n(), corrected = mean(corrected_t1), .groups = "drop")

# Same correction-rate curves split by error direction, for the two-facet Figure
# S14 (under-triage errors | over-triage errors). Benchmark level A cannot be
# under-triaged and level D cannot be over-triaged, so those lines are absent by
# construction.
error_correction_dir_df <- bind_rows(
  error_correction_df %>% filter(under_t) %>% mutate(error_type = "Under-triage errors"),
  error_correction_df %>% filter(over_t) %>% mutate(error_type = "Over-triage errors")
) %>%
  mutate(error_type = factor(error_type, levels = c("Under-triage errors", "Over-triage errors")))

error_correction_by_level_dir <- error_correction_dir_df %>%
  group_by(error_type, gold_triage, turn) %>%
  summarise(n = dplyr::n(), corrected = mean(corrected_t1), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    se = sqrt(pmax(corrected * (1 - corrected), 0) / pmax(n, 1)),
    lower = pmax(corrected - 1.96 * se, 0),
    upper = pmin(corrected + 1.96 * se, 1)
  )

error_correction_overall_dir <- error_correction_dir_df %>%
  group_by(error_type, turn) %>%
  summarise(n = dplyr::n(), corrected = mean(corrected_t1), .groups = "drop")

# Empirical correction-rate curves by turn for each error stratum (for the
# correction-rate stats reported in the belief-updating results and Figure S3).
correction_rate_by_turn <- function(df, label) {
  df %>%
    group_by(turn) %>%
    summarise(n = dplyr::n(), corrected = mean(corrected_t1), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(
      se = sqrt(pmax(corrected * (1 - corrected), 0) / pmax(n, 1)),
      lower = pmax(corrected - 1.96 * se, 0),
      upper = pmin(corrected + 1.96 * se, 1),
      stratum = label
    )
}

error_correction_curves <- bind_rows(
  correction_rate_by_turn(filter(error_correction_df, wrong_t), "Any error"),
  correction_rate_by_turn(filter(error_correction_df, under_t), "Under-triage"),
  correction_rate_by_turn(filter(error_correction_df, over_t), "Over-triage"),
  correction_rate_by_turn(filter(error_correction_df, emergency_under_t), "Emergency under-triage")
) %>%
  mutate(stratum = factor(stratum, levels = c("Any error", "Under-triage", "Over-triage", "Emergency under-triage")))

error_correction_palette <- c(
  "Any error" = "#111827",
  "Under-triage" = "#B91C1C",
  "Over-triage" = "#15803D",
  "Emergency under-triage" = "#7C3AED"
)

# Logistic mixed model for the inferential stat: per-turn change in the odds of
# correcting an existing error, adjusting for error depth and chatbot, with a
# per-conversation random intercept.
fit_correction_or <- function(df) {
  df <- df %>% filter(is.finite(turn))
  if (length(unique(df$turn)) < 2 || nrow(df) < 30 || sum(df$corrected_t1) < 5) {
    return(list(or = NA_real_, lower = NA_real_, upper = NA_real_, p = NA_real_, n = nrow(df)))
  }
  fit <- tryCatch(
    lme4::glmer(
      corrected_t1 ~ turn + abs_error_t + model_short + (1 | sim_id),
      data = df, family = binomial,
      control = lme4::glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e5))
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (is.null(fit)) {
    fit <- tryCatch(
      lme4::glmer(
        corrected_t1 ~ turn + abs_error_t + (1 | sim_id),
        data = df, family = binomial,
        control = lme4::glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e5))
      ),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
  }
  if (is.null(fit)) {
    return(list(or = NA_real_, lower = NA_real_, upper = NA_real_, p = NA_real_, n = nrow(df)))
  }
  co <- summary(fit)$coefficients
  est <- co["turn", "Estimate"]
  se <- co["turn", "Std. Error"]
  p <- co["turn", "Pr(>|z|)"]
  list(or = exp(est), lower = exp(est - 1.96 * se), upper = exp(est + 1.96 * se), p = p, n = nrow(df))
}

correction_or_any <- fit_correction_or(filter(error_correction_df, wrong_t))
correction_or_under <- fit_correction_or(filter(error_correction_df, under_t))
correction_or_over <- fit_correction_or(filter(error_correction_df, over_t))
correction_or_emerg <- fit_correction_or(filter(error_correction_df, emergency_under_t))

# Absolute correction probabilities at first vs last turn (for inline prose).
correction_curve_value <- function(label, which_turn) {
  d <- error_correction_curves %>% filter(stratum == label)
  tt <- if (which_turn == "first") min(d$turn) else max(d$turn)
  d$corrected[d$turn == tt][1]
}
correction_first_any <- correction_curve_value("Any error", "first")
correction_last_any <- correction_curve_value("Any error", "last")
correction_first_under <- correction_curve_value("Under-triage", "first")
correction_last_under <- correction_curve_value("Under-triage", "last")
correction_first_over <- correction_curve_value("Over-triage", "first")
correction_last_over <- correction_curve_value("Over-triage", "last")
correction_first_emerg <- correction_curve_value("Emergency under-triage", "first")
correction_last_emerg <- correction_curve_value("Emergency under-triage", "last")

# OR-per-turn formatter for inline reporting.
fmt_or_turn <- function(x) {
  if (is.na(x$or)) return("OR unavailable")
  paste0("OR ", sprintf("%.2f", x$or), " per turn, P ", fmt_p(x$p))
}
```

## Gap 1: Biased and self-consistent inferences

To test whether the way chatbots integrate accumulating information is linked with triage errors, we labeled every clinical feature the user simulator newly disclosed at each turn into one of six disclosure categories, including risk factors, clinical symptoms, precipitating or maintaining factors, predisposing factors, relevant negatives, and protective factors (see Methods). This labeling was reliable across 3 repeated classifications (Fleiss' κ = `r round(feature_category_kappa, 3)`). We then modeled each chatbot's current triage disposition as a function of the (i) the turn of the conversation; (ii) the counts of newly disclosed features in each of these six disclosure categories; (iii) its own triage disposition at the preceding turn; and (iv) the single-turn, full-disclosure triage disposition the chatbot assigned when handed all the facts in a single message.

Triage dispositions drifted toward higher urgency when the user disclosed a new risk factor (β = `r sprintf("%+.2f", anchoring_risk_coef)`, `r paste0("P ", fmt_p(anchoring_risk_p))`) or clinical symptom (β = `r sprintf("%+.2f", anchoring_symptom_coef)`, `r paste0("P ", fmt_p(anchoring_symptom_p))`). Protective factors had no detectable effect (β = `r sprintf("%+.2f", anchoring_protect_coef)`, `r paste0("P ", fmt_p(anchoring_protect_p))`). Only relevant negatives moved triage dispositions toward lower urgency (β = `r sprintf("%+.2f", anchoring_relneg_coef)`, `r paste0("P ", fmt_p(anchoring_relneg_p))`; Figure 3A). Set against the general over-triage bias evident across chatbots (Figure 3B), this asymmetry offers a mechanism for the progressive drift toward more urgent triage dispositions over the course of a conversation. 

At every turn, the chatbot was pulled both toward its running triage disposition (β = `r sprintf("%+.2f", anchoring_anchor_main_coef)`, `r paste0("P ", fmt_p(anchoring_anchor_main_p))`) and toward the corresponding single-turn disposition (β = `r sprintf("%+.2f", anchoring_oneshot_main_coef)`, `r paste0("P ", fmt_p(anchoring_oneshot_main_p))`). As conversations continued, current-turn dispositions increasingly repeated the preceding-turn disposition rather than the chatbot's own single-turn, full-disclosure judgment of the same case (Figure 3C): the pull toward the running disposition increased with each turn (β = `r sprintf("%+.3f", anchoring_anchor_turn_coef)` per turn, `r paste0("P ", fmt_p(anchoring_anchor_turn_p))`) while the pull toward the single-turn reference decreased (β = `r sprintf("%+.3f", anchoring_oneshot_turn_coef)` per turn, `r paste0("P ", fmt_p(anchoring_oneshot_turn_p))`). 

A consequence of this self-consistency bias[@kumaran_competing_2026] is that errors, once made, were rarely undone: across all errors, the chance of correcting a wrong disposition at the next turn fell from `r fmt_pct(correction_first_any)` early in the conversation to `r fmt_pct(correction_last_any)` by the end (`r fmt_or_turn(correction_or_any)`). Emergency under-triage was particularly hard to overturn, with only `r fmt_pct(correction_last_emerg)` of these errors  corrected by the final turn (Figure S3). These dynamics were unchanged when predictor (iv) — the chatbot's own single-turn, full-disclosure disposition — was replaced by the benchmark triage label, so that each running disposition was modeled against the true urgency level rather than the chatbot's own full-information judgment (Figure S4).

Together, these dynamics indicate that when triage information is revealed gradually rather than in a single message, chatbots tend to over-triage because their update probabilities are imbalanced, and find it increasingly hard to correct triage errors once they are made.


```{r}
#| label: figure_3_belief_updating
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 7
#| out-width: 100%
fig_anchor_a <- ggplot(anchoring_empirical_3a, aes(x = frac_match_oneshot, y = frac_match_prev, color = display_model)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey75", linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_path(aes(group = display_model), linewidth = 0.42, alpha = 0.45, lineend = "round") +
  geom_point(
    aes(fill = point_fill),
    shape = 21,
    size = manuscript_point_size * 0.90,
    stroke = 0.65,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_path(
    data = anchoring_empirical_3a_avg,
    aes(x = frac_match_oneshot, y = frac_match_prev, group = 1),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 1.05,
    alpha = 0.92,
    lineend = "round"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = anchoring_empirical_3a_avg,
    aes(x = frac_match_oneshot, y = frac_match_prev, fill = point_fill),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    shape = 21,
    color = "black",
    size = manuscript_point_size * 0.90,
    stroke = 0.75
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Fraction matching own single-turn judgment",
    y = "Self-consistency (matching preceding disposition)",
    title = "C",
    subtitle = "The running disposition outweighs the single-turn reference over turns"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

fig_anchor_b <- ggplot(anchoring_fact_df, aes(x = label, y = estimate, color = direction)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), linewidth = 0.75) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = direction), shape = 21, color = "black", size = manuscript_point_size * 0.9, stroke = 0.65) +
  scale_color_manual(values = anchoring_fact_direction_palette, name = NULL, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = anchoring_fact_direction_palette, name = NULL, guide = "none") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Effect of one new feature on current disposition",
    title = "A",
    subtitle = "User disclosures drive asymmetric triage updates"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = base_font_size * 0.72),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

fig_anchor_c <- ggplot(anchoring_attractor_df, aes(x = e_star, y = display_model, fill = display_model)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, xend = e_star, yend = display_model), color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.45) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, color = "black", size = manuscript_point_size, stroke = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = model_palette, guide = "none") +
  labs(
    x = "Model-implied net over-triage bias",
    y = NULL,
    title = "B",
    subtitle = "Most chatbots are biased toward over-triage"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = base_font_size * 0.72),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

# Left column stacks A (features) and B (per-chatbot bias), with per-item spacing
# matched across the two categorical panels; C (self-consistency) occupies the
# entire right column.
n_items_a <- nrow(anchoring_fact_df)
n_items_b <- nlevels(anchoring_attractor_df$display_model)
anchoring_left <- gridExtra::arrangeGrob(
  fig_anchor_b, fig_anchor_c,
  ncol = 1,
  heights = grid::unit(c(n_items_a, n_items_b), "null")
)
gridExtra::grid.arrange(anchoring_left, fig_anchor_a, ncol = 2, widths = c(1, 1.05))
```

**Figure 3.** Biased belief updating and self-consistency. The current triage disposition was modeled as a function of newly disclosed features in six disclosure categories, the model's own preceding triage disposition, and the same model's single-turn full-disclosure triage disposition for that case, with each effect varying by conversation turn and fixed intercepts for each chatbot. **A.** Effect of one additional newly disclosed feature of each category on the current triage disposition, adjusting for the preceding triage disposition and single-turn judgment; red indicates a shift toward greater urgency, green toward lower urgency, and grey non-significant effects, with 95% confidence intervals. **B.** Per-chatbot triage bias. Positive values indicate a net over-triage bias and negative values net under-triage. **C.** For each chatbot and conversation turn, the self-consistency of triage — the fraction of current-turn dispositions that repeated the preceding-turn triage disposition — plotted against the fraction that exactly matched the chatbot's own single-turn, full-disclosure disposition. Color denotes chatbot, lighter points represent earlier turns. The dashed line marks equal agreement with the previous triage disposition and the single-turn reference. As conversations lengthen, current-turn dispositions increasingly repeat the preceding triage disposition (points move upward).

## Gap 2: Rigid and perseverative context-seeking 

To test whether triage errors are linked with how chatbots seek information across multi-turn interactions, we designed and validated an LLM judge that coded every chatbot question into 15 clinician-defined question categories (Table S6; spanning self-harm and suicide, immediate danger, harm to others, safeguarding, mental state and psychotic symptoms, neurovegetative and physical symptoms, substance use, functioning, social support, treatment, history, and open elicitation). Coding was reliable across 3 repeated classifications (median Fleiss' κ = `r round(question_median_kappa, 3)`), and nearly all questions were covered by the taxonomy (only `r fmt_pct(question_zero_code_rate)` had no question code, while `r fmt_pct(question_multicode_rate)` received more than one).

The question a chatbot selected was strongly associated with the triage disposition it assumed at that turn. When a chatbot's running disposition was routine (A), it asked more open, exploratory questions (`r fmt_pct(question_pred_a_oe)` versus `r fmt_pct(question_pred_d_oe)` open elicitation at D, P `r question_pred_oe_ad_p`). Conversely, when its current disposition was an emergency (D), it shifted toward immediate danger (`r fmt_pct(question_pred_d_im)` versus `r fmt_pct(question_pred_a_im)`, P `r question_pred_im_ad_p`), physical health (`r fmt_pct(question_pred_d_ph)` versus `r fmt_pct(question_pred_a_ph)`, P `r question_pred_ph_ad_p`), and safeguarding (`r fmt_pct(question_pred_d_sg)` versus `r fmt_pct(question_pred_a_sg)`, P `r question_pred_sg_ad_p`; Figure S5A). Please note that question and triage calls to the target AI chatbot were issued independently: the question model could not see the provisional triage label, and the link between them arose only through the shared conversation transcript. 

```{r}
#| label: figure3_question_phenotyping_data
#| include: false
question_code_short_labels <- c(
  "SI" = "Suicidal ideation",
  "SP" = "Safety / means",
  "HI" = "Harm to others",
  "SG" = "Safeguarding",
  "NV" = "Neurovegetative",
  "MS" = "Mental state",
  "NS" = "Neuro / organic",
  "SU" = "Substance use",
  "PH" = "Physical health",
  "RF" = "Functioning",
  "SS" = "Social support",
  "TX" = "Treatment",
  "HX" = "History",
  "OE" = "Open elicitation",
  "IM" = "Immediacy"
)

question_code_axis_labels <- setNames(
  paste0(question_code_order, " (", question_code_short_labels[question_code_order], ")"),
  question_code_order
)

question_code_turn_order <- question_long %>%
  group_by(code, turn) %>%
  summarise(n = sum(present, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(code) %>%
  summarise(
    total = sum(n),
    mean_turn = if_else(total > 0, sum(n * turn) / total, 3),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(mean_turn) %>%
  pull(code)

question_turn_prevalence_df <- question_long %>%
  group_by(turn, code) %>%
  summarise(prevalence = mean(present), .groups = "drop")

compute_question_turn_loading <- function(df, split_vars = character()) {
  group_count_vars <- c(split_vars, "code", "turn")
  group_total_vars <- c(split_vars, "code")
  
  df %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(group_count_vars))) %>%
    summarise(n = sum(present, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(group_total_vars))) %>%
    tidyr::complete(turn = seq_len(max_turn), fill = list(n = 0)) %>%
    mutate(
      total = sum(n, na.rm = TRUE),
      loading = if_else(total > 0, n / total, NA_real_)
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(
      turn_label = factor(turn, levels = seq_len(max_turn)),
      code_label = factor(
        question_code_axis_labels[code],
        levels = rev(question_code_axis_labels[question_code_turn_order])
      )
    )
}

question_turn_predicted_loading <- question_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(current_predicted_triage)) %>%
  compute_question_turn_loading("current_predicted_triage")

question_turn_model_prevalence <- question_long %>%
  group_by(display_model, turn, code) %>%
  summarise(prevalence = mean(present, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

question_turn_summary <- question_turn_model_prevalence %>%
  group_by(turn, code) %>%
  summarise(mean_ci_95(prevalence), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    code_facet_label = factor(
      question_code_axis_labels[code],
      levels = question_code_axis_labels[question_code_turn_order]
    )
  )

question_turn_predicted_model_prevalence <- question_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(current_predicted_triage)) %>%
  group_by(display_model, current_predicted_triage, turn, code) %>%
  summarise(prevalence = mean(present, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

question_turn_predicted_summary <- question_turn_predicted_model_prevalence %>%
  group_by(current_predicted_triage, turn, code) %>%
  summarise(mean_ci_95(prevalence), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    code_label = factor(
      question_code_axis_labels[code],
      levels = rev(question_code_axis_labels[question_code_turn_order])
    ),
    code_facet_label = factor(
      question_code_axis_labels[code],
      levels = question_code_axis_labels[question_code_turn_order]
    )
  )

question_loading_limits <- c(0, 0.35)

plot_question_loading_heatmap <- function(
    df,
    facet_var,
    subtitle,
    show_legend = TRUE,
    show_values = TRUE,
    facet_labeller = label_value,
    facet_nrow = NULL) {
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(text_color = viridis_text_color(loading, question_loading_limits))
  
  facet_layer <- if (is.null(facet_nrow)) {
    facet_grid(cols = vars(!!rlang::sym(facet_var)), labeller = facet_labeller)
  } else {
    facet_wrap(vars(!!rlang::sym(facet_var)), nrow = facet_nrow, labeller = facet_labeller)
  }
  
  ggplot(
    df,
    aes(x = turn_label, y = code_label, fill = loading)
  ) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.35) +
    {
      if (show_values) {
        geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", loading), color = text_color), size = heatmap_label_size)
      }
    } +
    facet_layer +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(
      option = "D",
      limits = question_loading_limits,
      oob = scales::squish,
      labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
      name = "P(turn | code)"
    ) +
    scale_color_identity(guide = "none") +
    labs(
      x = NULL,
      y = NULL,
      title = NULL,
      subtitle = subtitle
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
    theme(
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = base_font_size * 0.72),
      legend.position = if (show_legend) "bottom" else "none",
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
}

plot_question_loading_bubbles <- function(
    df,
    facet_var,
    subtitle,
    show_legend = TRUE,
    show_values = TRUE,
    facet_labeller = label_value,
    facet_nrow = NULL,
    x_text_size = base_font_size * 0.72) {
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      bubble_label = if_else(!is.na(loading) & loading >= 0.01, sprintf("%.0f", 100 * loading), ""),
      text_color = viridis_text_color(loading, question_loading_limits)
    )

  facet_layer <- if (is.null(facet_nrow)) {
    facet_grid(cols = vars(!!rlang::sym(facet_var)), labeller = facet_labeller)
  } else {
    facet_wrap(vars(!!rlang::sym(facet_var)), nrow = facet_nrow, labeller = facet_labeller)
  }

  ggplot(df, aes(x = turn_label, y = code_label)) +
    geom_point(
      aes(size = loading, fill = loading),
      shape = 21,
      color = "black",
      stroke = 0.26,
      alpha = 0.90
    ) +
    {
      if (show_values) {
        geom_text(aes(label = bubble_label, color = text_color), size = heatmap_label_size * 0.82, fontface = "bold")
      }
    } +
    facet_layer +
    scale_size_area(
      max_size = 7.5,
      limits = question_loading_limits,
      oob = scales::squish,
      labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
      name = "P(turn | code)"
    ) +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(
      option = "D",
      limits = question_loading_limits,
      oob = scales::squish,
      labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
      name = "P(turn | code)"
    ) +
    scale_color_identity(guide = "none") +
    labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, title = NULL, subtitle = subtitle) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey88", linewidth = 0.22),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = x_text_size),
      legend.position = if (show_legend) "bottom" else "none",
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
    ) +
    guides(
      size = guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE),
      fill = "none"
    )
}
```
