**Figure S2.** Information disclosure across conversational triage. **A.** Fraction of triage-discriminative facts disclosed at each conversational turn. Colored lines represent individual chatbots and the black line their average; the dotted line marks the single-turn, full-disclosure ceiling. Disclosure rose fastest over the first few turns and plateaued thereafter, reaching a median of `r fmt_pct(disclosure_final_median)` (IQR `r fmt_pct(disclosure_final_q1)`–`r fmt_pct(disclosure_final_q3)`) of each case's facts by the final turn. **B.** Triage error versus disclosure at the vignette level (one point per vignette, averaged across chatbots), colored by benchmark triage label; positive values indicate over-triage and negative under-triage, and the dashed line marks perfect calibration. Across vignettes, the amount disclosed did not predict the direction of triage error (`r disclosure_error_vignette_cor_label`). **C.** Fraction of triage-relevant facts disclosed by the final turn, grouped by whether the final prediction was correct, under-triaged, or over-triaged, shown separately for benchmark levels A–D. Colored points are individual chatbots; black points and ranges show the mean and 95% CI. Final-turn disclosure did not differ by triage outcome at the routine, intermediate, and emergency levels (A, B, D), with the exception of urgent cases (C), where under-triaged conversations had disclosed less triage-relevant information. **D.** Fraction of triage-discriminative facts disclosed in the two triage settings. Round markers indicate disclosure by the final conversational turn, and triangle markers indicate disclosure in the single-turn benchmark. Colored points are individual chatbots; black points and ranges show the mean and 95% CI across chatbots. The single-turn message discloses `r fmt_pct(one_shot_disclosure_overall_mean)` of discriminative facts on average versus `r fmt_pct(disclosure_final_mean)` by the final conversational turn, a gap of roughly `r fmt_pct(disclosure_setting_gap_mean)` of triage-relevant information the conversation left unelicited. **E.** Per-vignette disclosure gap (single-turn minus conversational final-turn disclosure) versus accuracy gap (single-turn minus conversational triage correctness), averaged across chatbots and colored by benchmark triage label. Across vignettes the information disclosure and accuracy gaps were unrelated (`r disclosure_gap_vignette_cor_label`).

## Figure S3

```{r}
#| label: figure_s3_error_correction
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4.5
#| out-width: 100%
fig_s14_correction <- ggplot(
  error_correction_by_level_dir,
  aes(x = turn, y = corrected, color = gold_triage, fill = gold_triage, group = gold_triage)
) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), color = NA, alpha = 0.12) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.75) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, color = "black", size = manuscript_point_size * 0.7, stroke = 0.35) +
  geom_line(
    data = error_correction_overall_dir,
    aes(x = turn, y = corrected, group = 1),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "black", linewidth = 1.05, lineend = "round"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = error_correction_overall_dir,
    aes(x = turn, y = corrected),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    shape = 21, fill = "black", color = "black",
    size = manuscript_point_size * 0.7, stroke = 0.35
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ error_type) +
  scale_color_manual(values = triage_assumption_palette, breaks = triage_levels, labels = triage_labels_short, name = "Benchmark triage") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = triage_assumption_palette, breaks = triage_levels, labels = triage_labels_short, name = "Benchmark triage") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = scales::breaks_width(2)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, NA)) +
  labs(
    x = "Turn (t)",
    y = "P(error corrected at t+1)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(size = base_font_size * 0.85),
    legend.text = element_text(size = base_font_size * 0.78),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_font_size * 0.9)
  )
fig_s14_correction
```

**Figure S3.** Correctability of conversational triage errors over turns. Among transitions where a chatbot's running triage disposition was wrong, the probability that the error was corrected to the benchmark label at the next turn, split into under-triage errors (left) and over-triage errors (right) and shown separately for benchmark triage levels A–D (colored lines, 95% CI bands); the black line is the average across all errors of that type. Benchmark level A cannot be under-triaged and level D cannot be over-triaged, so those lines are absent by construction. Once a disposition was wrong it became progressively less likely to be corrected as the conversation lengthened: the probability of correcting an under-triage error at the next turn fell from `r fmt_pct(correction_first_under)` early in the conversation to `r fmt_pct(correction_last_under)` by the end (`r fmt_or_turn(correction_or_under)`), and the probability of correcting an over-triage error fell from `r fmt_pct(correction_first_over)` to `r fmt_pct(correction_last_over)` (`r fmt_or_turn(correction_or_over)`). Emergency under-triage was rarely corrected at any turn, with only `r fmt_pct(correction_last_emerg)` corrected by the final turn (`r fmt_or_turn(correction_or_emerg)`).

## Figure S4

```{r}
#| label: figure_s4_belief_updating_gold
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 7
#| out-width: 100%
fig_s4_c <- ggplot(anchoring_gold_empirical_3a, aes(x = frac_match_gold, y = frac_match_prev, color = display_model)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey75", linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_path(aes(group = display_model), linewidth = 0.42, alpha = 0.45, lineend = "round") +
  geom_point(
    aes(fill = point_fill),
    shape = 21,
    size = manuscript_point_size * 0.90,
    stroke = 0.65,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_path(
    data = anchoring_gold_empirical_3a_avg,
    aes(x = frac_match_gold, y = frac_match_prev, group = 1),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 1.05,
    alpha = 0.92,
    lineend = "round"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = anchoring_gold_empirical_3a_avg,
    aes(x = frac_match_gold, y = frac_match_prev, fill = point_fill),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    shape = 21,
    color = "black",
    size = manuscript_point_size * 0.90,
    stroke = 0.75
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Fraction matching benchmark label",
    y = "Self-consistency (matching preceding disposition)",
    title = "C",
    subtitle = "Triage follows the running disposition more than the benchmark"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

fig_s4_a <- ggplot(anchoring_gold_fact_df, aes(x = label, y = estimate, color = direction)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), linewidth = 0.75) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = direction), shape = 21, color = "black", size = manuscript_point_size * 0.9, stroke = 0.65) +
  scale_color_manual(values = anchoring_fact_direction_palette, name = NULL, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = anchoring_fact_direction_palette, name = NULL, guide = "none") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Effect of one new feature on current disposition",
    title = "A",
    subtitle = "User disclosures drive asymmetric triage updates"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = base_font_size * 0.72),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

fig_s4_b <- ggplot(anchoring_gold_attractor_df, aes(x = e_star, y = display_model, fill = display_model)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, xend = e_star, yend = display_model), color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.45) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, color = "black", size = manuscript_point_size, stroke = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = model_palette, guide = "none") +
  labs(
    x = "Model-implied net over-triage bias",
    y = NULL,
    title = "B",
    subtitle = "Most chatbots are biased toward over-triage"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = base_font_size * 0.72),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

# Layout mirrors Figure 3: left column stacks A (features) and B (per-chatbot
# bias); C (self-consistency) occupies the right column.
n_items_a_gold <- nrow(anchoring_gold_fact_df)
n_items_b_gold <- nlevels(anchoring_gold_attractor_df$display_model)
anchoring_gold_left <- gridExtra::arrangeGrob(
  fig_s4_a, fig_s4_b,
  ncol = 1,
  heights = grid::unit(c(n_items_a_gold, n_items_b_gold), "null")
)
gridExtra::grid.arrange(anchoring_gold_left, fig_s4_c, ncol = 2, widths = c(1, 1.05))
```

**Figure S4.** Biased belief updating benchmarked against ground truth. The current triage disposition was modeled as a function of newly disclosed features in six disclosure categories, the model's own preceding triage disposition, and the benchmark triage label for that case, with each effect varying by conversation turn and fixed intercepts for each chatbot. **A.** Effect of one additional newly disclosed feature of each category on the current triage disposition, adjusting for the preceding triage disposition and the benchmark label; red indicates a shift toward greater urgency, green toward lower urgency, and grey not significant, with 95% confidence intervals. Replicating the main analysis, a newly disclosed risk factor (β = `r sprintf("%+.2f", anchoring_gold_risk_coef)`, `r paste0("P ", fmt_p(anchoring_gold_risk_p))`) or clinical symptom (β = `r sprintf("%+.2f", anchoring_gold_symptom_coef)`, `r paste0("P ", fmt_p(anchoring_gold_symptom_p))`) shifted dispositions toward greater urgency, only relevant negatives lowered urgency (β = `r sprintf("%+.2f", anchoring_gold_relneg_coef)`, `r paste0("P ", fmt_p(anchoring_gold_relneg_p))`), and protective factors had no detectable effect (β = `r sprintf("%+.2f", anchoring_gold_protect_coef)`, `r paste0("P ", fmt_p(anchoring_gold_protect_p))`). **B.** Per-chatbot triage bias. Positive values indicate a net over-triage bias and negative values net under-triage. **C.** For each chatbot and conversation turn, the self-consistency of triage — the fraction of current-turn dispositions that exactly repeated the preceding-turn triage disposition — plotted against the fraction that exactly matched the benchmark label. Each point is one chatbot at one turn; color denotes chatbot, lighter points represent earlier turns. The dashed line marks equal agreement with the preceding triage disposition and the benchmark label. As conversations lengthen, current-turn dispositions increasingly repeat the preceding triage disposition (points move upward): as in the main analysis, the pull toward the running disposition increased with each turn (β = `r sprintf("%+.3f", anchoring_gold_anchor_turn_coef)` per turn, `r paste0("P ", fmt_p(anchoring_gold_anchor_turn_p))`) while the pull toward the benchmark label decreased (β = `r sprintf("%+.3f", anchoring_gold_gold_turn_coef)` per turn, `r paste0("P ", fmt_p(anchoring_gold_gold_turn_p))`).

```{r}

figure_s15_lower <- gridExtra::arrangeGrob(
  fig4a,
  fig3c_disclosure_update,
  ncol = 2,
  widths = c(2, 1)

gridExtra::grid.arrange(
```

**Figure S5.** Information sampled during conversational triage, organized by the chatbot's current triage estimate. **A.** Prevalence of each of the 15 question categories across conversation turns, shown separately by the chatbot's current triage disposition (the provisional disposition it holds when asking, before the simulated user replies). Colored lines show the mean for each current triage disposition (A–D), the black line the overall mean, and ribbons 95% CIs. **B.** Probability that each question type was followed by newly disclosed information of a given disclosure category, shown separately by the chatbot's current triage estimate. Larger circles indicate that new information was disclosed more often. **C.** Timing and direction of triage updates after new information appeared; larger circles indicate more frequent disclosure, values above zero indicate a shift toward greater urgency and values below zero toward lower urgency, after adjustment for turn, target chatbot, presentation, and question type. Turn 1 is the opening message and therefore has no preceding model question.

## Figure S7

```{r}
#| label: figure_s7_model_leaderboards
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 13
#| fig-height: 9.5
#| out-width: 100%

# Model leaderboards. Six per-model rankings on axes relevant to the paper's
# findings, all drawn from dataframes already constructed above. Lollipops share the
# global model palette so that a given chatbot keeps its colour across panels.
lb_final_acc <- llm_turn_df %>%
  filter(turn == max_turn) %>%
  group_by(display_model) %>%
  summarise(value = mean(triage_match, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

lb_signed_error <- llm_turn_df %>%
  filter(turn == max_turn) %>%
  group_by(display_model) %>%
  summarise(
    value = mean(signed_error, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

lb_d_under <- llm_turn_df %>%
  filter(turn == max_turn, gold_triage == "D") %>%
  group_by(display_model) %>%
  summarise(value = mean(signed_error < 0, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

lb_ab_over <- llm_turn_df %>%
  filter(turn == max_turn, gold_triage %in% c("A", "B")) %>%
  group_by(display_model) %>%
  summarise(value = mean(signed_error > 0, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

lb_persev <- rigidity_df %>%
  mutate(display_model = factor(display_model(as.character(target_model_label)), levels = names(model_palette))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(display_model)) %>%
  group_by(display_model) %>%
  summarise(value = mean(perseveration, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

lb_correction <- error_correction_df %>%
  filter(wrong_t) %>%
  mutate(display_model = factor(display_model, levels = names(model_palette))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(display_model)) %>%
  group_by(display_model) %>%
  summarise(value = mean(corrected_t1, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

make_leaderboard <- function(df, panel_letter, metric_title, higher_better = TRUE, diverging = FALSE, ordinal = FALSE) {
  d <- df %>% filter(!is.na(display_model), !is.na(value))
  ord <- if ("sort_key" %in% names(d)) -d$sort_key else if (higher_better) d$value else -d$value
  d <- d %>% mutate(model_ord = fct_reorder(display_model, ord))
  if (diverging) {
    d <- d %>% mutate(lab = if (ordinal) sprintf("%+.2f", value) else sprintf("%+.0f", value * 100))
    hj <- ifelse(d$value >= 0, -0.2, 1.2)
    expand_mult <- c(0.16, 0.16)
  } else {
    d <- d %>% mutate(lab = sprintf("%.0f%%", value * 100))
    hj <- -0.2
    expand_mult <- c(0.02, 0.2)
  }
  p <- ggplot(d, aes(x = model_ord, y = value))
  if (diverging) {
    p <- p + geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.3, color = "grey40")
  }
  p +
    geom_segment(aes(x = model_ord, xend = model_ord, y = 0, yend = value, color = display_model),
                 linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_point(aes(fill = display_model), shape = 21, color = "black",
               size = manuscript_point_size * 1.1, stroke = 0.5) +
    geom_text(aes(label = lab), hjust = hj, size = base_font_size * 0.32) +
    scale_color_manual(values = model_palette, guide = "none", drop = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = model_palette, guide = "none", drop = FALSE) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = expand_mult)) +
    coord_flip(clip = "off") +
    labs(title = panel_letter, subtitle = metric_title, x = NULL, y = NULL) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = base_font_size * 0.95),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x = element_blank()
    )
}

lb_a <- make_leaderboard(lb_final_acc, "A", "Final-turn conversational accuracy", higher_better = TRUE)
lb_b <- make_leaderboard(lb_signed_error, "B", "Triage error at final turn", diverging = TRUE, ordinal = TRUE)
lb_c <- make_leaderboard(lb_d_under, "C", "Emergency (D) under-triage rate", higher_better = FALSE)
lb_d <- make_leaderboard(lb_ab_over, "D", "Over-triage rate (levels A/B)", higher_better = FALSE)
lb_e <- make_leaderboard(lb_persev, "E", "Perseveration", higher_better = FALSE)
lb_f <- make_leaderboard(lb_correction, "F", "Error-correction rate", higher_better = TRUE)

gridExtra::grid.arrange(lb_a, lb_b, lb_c, lb_d, lb_e, lb_f, ncol = 2)
```

**Figure S7.** Model leaderboards. Each panel ranks the `r n_models` evaluated chatbots on one axis relevant to the paper's findings, colored by the shared model palette so that a given model keeps its color throughout. **A.** Final-turn conversational accuracy against the benchmark triage label (higher is better). **B.** Mean triage error at the final turn, with models ordered by triage error, from most over-triage (top) to most under-triage (bottom). **C.** Under-triage rate for emergency (level D) cases at the final turn (lower is safer). **D.** Over-triage rate for lower-acuity (levels A and B) cases at the final turn (lower is better). **E.** Mean per-conversation perseveration, the tendency to repeat one line of questioning rather than broaden context-seeking (lower reflects more flexible questioning). **F.** Error-correction rate: among turns where the running triage disposition was already wrong, the fraction corrected to the benchmark label at the following turn (higher is better).
